purpose: Distinguishes between genuinely stable and fragile low-vol environments that produce identical vol surfaces but have radically different risk profiles and transition probabilities.
components: Compute: (Government Deficit/GDP) minus (Change in Private Debt/GDP)
Type A (positive value): Kalecki profit-supported — fiscal flows sustain corporate earnings, genuinely stable low vol
Type B (negative value): Minsky credit-supported — private credit expansion drives earnings and low vol, structurally fragile
Historical calibration: 2010-2013 and 2013-2017 = Type A; 2005-2007 and 2018-2019 = Type B
current_assessment: Current 6-7% fiscal deficit suggests Type A (profit-supported), but simultaneous growth in private credit ($1.7T+) creates mixed signals. The diagnostic may show a transitional state between pure types.
limitations: Real-time data has 2-3 month publication lag (Federal Reserve Z.1)
Unvalidated for non-U.S. economies with different fiscal rules
